Them: Kind of last minute. But I had mentioned this on our previous

So that\'s why I wanted to bring him in, because I thought it was

Me: Oh, man. Yeah, I know. Yeah. I really hope it isn\'t one of those

Them: Yeah. Yeah, it\'s gonna be nice.

Me: No, I\'m really excited for this. As I said to you guys, been

Them: Yeah.

Me: They are, I believe, a six person startup, light speed backed. More

which is a little bit of an abstract thing, until you realize and
that\'s the basis of the talk we\'re doing on Monday or sorry,
Wednesday. Logan is actually like, why do AI apps so often let us down
and feel more overwhelming than not? My belief is that it\'s because we
are used to a store and capture or sorry, capture and store paradigm
when it comes to information, as opposed to a sort of like, filter and
synthesize, which is in the age of AI. With 10 times as much data and
information, you are essentially 10 times as overwhelmed at all times.
And I\'ve spoken to a lot of founders and VCs recently that have
essentially said, hey, things like slip through the cracks. It\'s sort
of constant. I don\'t know what to be doing at any given point of the
day. And you just generally feel like you should be achieving a lot and
actually are achieving very little. So all of this hopefully resonates.
And so the cool thing about Zoe is it may not be the answer to your
problems. And it may not be the answer yet, but I think it\'s a very
cool model for what computing could or should be like in a more sort of
prosumer way. So one thing I\'ll start off with, or I\'ll bring this
example up later today, because Gabby actually asked why can I request
sharing? Gabby actually asked why Zoe uses markdown files, but you\'ll
see in a second that it\'s.

Okay, I need to look up this term amongst the files. These are related

the folks here, but with this, you could literally tell it. Hey,
weather.com\'s API is free. I want you to go there. I want you to tap
it, and I want you to give me the data. And this is the data that I
want, and it\'ll just do it.

Me: Yes. So without revealing my secret key necessarily. There is a

To not care about these principles when we had no power, right when I

You already have great ideas. We already think of cool stuff. And the
problem is that it\'s so hard to act on it because all the information
just ends up pooled in different places. So, yeah, long story short.
